kitchen.title: “My Pulled Pork” + Mushrooms + Sausage 🍕 In The Kitchen W/ Unique Starr #TheKitchenCouchPodcast
In The Kitchen w/ Unique Starr: The Impact Of Technology On The Youth
kitchen.description: In this conversation, Dav Lewis and guest, Unique Starr, discusse her journey through the Harvard Social Impact Initiative, grappling with feelings of shame and identity as he navigates the intersection of elite education and his commitment to his community. They delve into the impact of technology on children, particularly regarding screen addiction, and Unique Starr shares her personal experiences as a parent who has chosen to limit technology for his daughter. The discussion also touches on food choices, cultural identity, and the challenges of cooking and meal prep in a busy life, highlighting the importance of being mindful about what we consume and how it shapes our lives.

kitchen.title: Part I: In The Kitchen w/Tatiuana Holland: Justice & Forgiveness |
kitchen.description:
In this episode Dav and special guest, Tatiuana Holland, explore a range of topics including cooking (Oyster Mushrooms + Dirty Rice), Louisiana roots, academic journey, legal career, the psychology of law, fairness in the legal system, collaborative justice, the criminal justice system, forgiveness, and self-healing. The discussion delves into the concept of collaborative justice and the complexities of forgiveness and self-healing.

Takeaways
* Collaborative justice
* Forgiveness and self-healing

kitchen.title: TASTE TEST W/ JAAG (PLANT-BASED ENCHILADAS)
kitchen.description: Jaag get's his first sample of Dav's famous #Plantbased Enchilada Casserole, Black Beans (spicy), and cauliflower mexican style rice. Full Episode Available on YouTube Channel.

kitchen.title: IN THE KITCHEN W: BREAKING THE CYCLE JAAG
kitchen.description: In this segment, Dav and special guest, Jaag, delve in the power of surrendering after pleading with God for a 2nd Chance. What lead to his transformation/change and breaking generational cycles on his journey to self-discovery through Islamic practices.

kitchen.title: Welcome to our live show! In The Kitchen With Jaag (ThinkFitCo)
kitchen.description:
In this episode, Dav Lewis, discusses Jaag’s journey to self-discovery, wellness, self-love, care, family, and his vision for the future. The featured dish for this episode is plant-based enchilada casserole with black beans and cauliflower rice. 
In this episode, Dav Lewis delves into Jaag’s transformative journey towards self-discovery, wellness, self-love, care, family, and his aspirations for the future. The featured dish of the episode is a delectable plant-based enchilada casserole, crafted with black beans and cauliflower rice.

kitchen.title: In The Kitchen w/ Marvin Valcin: Special Episode Dedicated To Dav's Mother.
kitchen.description:
In this special episode of The Kitchen Couch, Host Dav Lewis honors the memory of his mother, who transitioned on this day — December 11, 2022. Her life, love, and legacy deeply shaped his journey toward healing, wellness, and compassion, inspiring the very heart of this podcast.

Dav opens up about his personal path into plant-based eating, rooted in his own health experiences and a commitment to educating others. He explores the importance of understanding nutrition, making balanced dietary choices, and offers practical, accessible tips for anyone looking to transition into a healthier lifestyle.

This episode also celebrates the power of community, family, and shared meals, highlighting how cooking together can foster connection, support well-being, and honor the people who continue to guide us from beyond.

couch.title: Pluto Is In Our Charts To Teach Us About Power and Attachments #TheKitchenCouchPodcast #UniqueSatarr
Think Dep
couch.description: Thanks for joining our exclusive live broadcast. Feel free to share your questions and interact with other participants in the chat.

couch.title: Part III: Give Me A Year By Shonda Scott - What Is Love?
couch.description:
In Part III of the episode, Shonda Scott delves deeper into the themes of her book 'Give Me A Year,' focusing on the personal transformations that occur when individuals commit to embracing change. She shares more personal anecdotes and discusses the challenges and rewards of stepping out of one's comfort zone. The conversation emphasizes the importance of resilience, the power of storytelling, and the impact of community involvement in personal growth. Shonda also highlights the role of self-reflection and mindfulness in navigating life's transitions.

Key Takeaways:

Embracing change leads to personal transformation.
Resilience is key to overcoming challenges.
Storytelling can inspire and connect people.
Community involvement enhances personal growth.
Self-reflection aids in navigating transitions.

couch.title: GIVE ME A YEAR WITH SHONDA SCOTT: PART 1
couch.description:
In this powerful and timely episode of The Kitchen Couch, host @DavLewis sits down with @Shonda Scott —businesswoman, author, mother, community advocate, and changemaker—for a deeply honest conversation about **change, purpose, healing, and community responsibility**.

Shonda shares the heart behind her book, *Give Me a Year: 12 New Things to Embrace Change and Live Your Best Life*, and how the “12 New Things” journey became more than a challenge—it became a lifestyle rooted in courage, growth, and self-accountability. Together, Dav and Shonda explore why fear keeps so many of us stuck, how comfort can block our blessings, and why real transformation requires doing the work—without shortcuts.

The conversation also weaves through critical topics including:

* Embracing vulnerability and storytelling as tools for healing
* Why change is inevitable—and resisting it only limits us
* The impact of the pandemic on mindset, mental health, and community
* Generational differences, social media illusions, and the danger of “microwave success”
* The responsibility we have to pour back into our communities, especially around food insecurity and food deserts

At its core, this episode is a reminder that **growth is a process**, discomfort is part of the journey, and change—while hard—is where freedom lives. Whether you’re feeling stuck, seeking purpose, or ready to say “yes” to something new, this conversation will challenge and inspire you to take the next step.

🎙️ **The Kitchen Couch** — feeding the soul one conversation at a time.
📚 Featuring Shonda Scott, author of *Give Me a Year*.

couch.title: Part II: 2 X Grammy Nominated @darrelwallsmusic "i DO NOT CARE" - LOVE, SELF-CARE, CHURCH, MUSIC
couch.description:
PART II: ON THE KITCHEN COUCH WITH MR. DARREL WALLS -The conversation delves into the importance of self-care, setting boundaries, and the power of saying no. It explores the impact of reading, self-reflection, and the development of the mind. Additionally, it discusses the significance of being present, balancing career and self-care, and the impact of establishing boundaries for personal well-being. The conversation concludes with a thought-provoking discussion on superpowers and human nature. The conversation delves into the relativity of right and wrong, the impact of judgment and understanding, the importance of love and humility, the role of the church in society, and the pursuit of joy over happiness. It explores cultural differences, divides in beliefs, misrepresentation of God's love, and the need for education and understanding. The discussion also touches on humility, representation of God's love, and the daily practice of grounding and preparation.

Takeaways
- Self-care through boundaries
- The power of saying no Relativity of right and wrong
- The impact of judgment and understanding
- The importance of love and humility
- The role of the church in society
- The pursuit of joy over happiness

Chapters
- 00:00 Introduction to the Podcast
- 54:36 The Importance of Dating Yourself
- 08:30 Yoga and Being Present
- 10:22 Balancing Career and Self-Care
- 29:18 The Impact of Saying No and Establishing Boundaries
- 19:52 Fun Questions and Insights

couch.title: Special Christmas Edition Part I: On The Couch With Darrel Walls
couch.description:
In Part 1 of "On the Couch with Darrel Walls," Dav Lewis hosts an engaging conversation with his close friend and two-time Grammy-nominated vocalist, Darrel Walls. The episode delves into themes of personal growth, self-discovery, and the importance of understanding and empathy in relationships. Darrel shares insights from his journey, emphasizing the evolving nature of one's purpose and the significance of self-care and setting boundaries. The discussion also highlights Darrel's new EP, "Heart Of Mine," which has earned him two Grammy nominations. Throughout the episode, the warmth and camaraderie between Dav and Darrel shine through, offering listeners a heartfelt and thought-provoking experience.

Takeaways
- Evolution of Purpose
- Navigating Disagreements Self-love and self-discovery
- The concept of love and sacrifice

Chapters
- 00:00 Introduction to the Podcast
- 06:18 Plant-Based Lifestyle and Health
- 18:14 Social Media and Communication
- 28:17 Evolution of Purpose
- 38:00 Vulnerability and Love
- 43:02 Understanding Love and Sacrifice
- 58:14 Dating Yourself and Relationships

couch.title: Tamera Fair - The Spice Of Life
couch.description: In this episode of The Kitchen Couch, host Dav Lewis welcomes Tamera Fair, a multifaceted entrepreneur, actress, author, and advocate. They discuss the importance of food in community and culture, share personal stories about childhood food memories, and explore themes of health, faith, love, and forgiveness. Tamera opens up about her health challenges and the journey of self-discovery, emphasizing the power of love and the importance of listening to one's body. The conversation culminates in reflections on personal growth and the advice Tamara would give to her younger self.

couch.title: Episode 2: Llloyd Gite - From The News Room To The Gallery
couch.description:
This week on  @TheKitchenCouchPodcast  we welcome special guest, Lloyd Gite, former journalist and now owner of the Gite Gallery. 

In this engaging conversation, Dav Lewis and Lloyd Gite explore the intersection of art, culture, and community. Lloyd shares his journey from a successful journalism career to becoming a prominent art gallery owner in Houston. They discuss the importance of art in reconnecting individuals with their heritage, the challenges faced in the art business, and the evolving appreciation of art within the community. The dialogue also touches on broader societal issues, including the state of journalism, leadership, and the significance of love and legacy in one's life.

couch.title: Episode 2: Featuring Lloyd Gite On @TheKitchenCouchPodcast  Tomorrow at 12 PM CST
couch.description: Mr. Gite discusses “gusto” and a never stop attitude as well as compliments Dav on the food that was prepared.

couch.title: Episode 1: Lanell Johnson: Do The Work
couch.description: In this episode of Kitchen Couch, host Dav Lewis engages in a heartfelt conversation with real estate agent Lanell Johnson (LMJ Properties). They discuss the importance of community support during challenging times, the significance of self-care and mental health, and the impact of personal trauma on relationships. Linnell shares her journey in real estate, emphasizing the need for excellence in customer service and the importance of counseling in navigating life's challenges. The conversation also touches on food culture, diet choices, and the power of gratitude in daily life.

couch.title: Episode 1- Taste Test: Plant-Based Jambalya
couch.description: Dav and Lanell discuss the inspiration behind his plant-based and ingredients.
